title: Alpha Engine Long-Format Outcome Record (eval-storage contract, v1)
description: One resolved evaluation outcome for a (signal, score_date, horizon_days) triple — the long-format replacement for the wide horizon-suffixed score_performance columns (beat_spy_5d/beat_spy_21d/spy_21d_return/log_alpha_21d/...). Root-cause fix for the config#1456 silent-starvation bug class (EPIC config#1483): with outcomes stored one-row-per-horizon and consumers filtering WHERE horizon_days=:h (h resolved from HorizonPolicy in nousergon_lib.quant.horizons), adding or changing a diagnostic horizon becomes a DATA change (zero renames) and an unproduced horizon returns empty rows (graceful + detectable) instead of a dark hardcoded column. Produced across research.db (score_performance_outcomes table), the S3 outcome artifact (long-format array), and ArcticDB (one row per signal/date/horizon). Field names mirror nousergon_lib.quant.horizons.OutcomeColumns so the Phase-2 dual-write is an unambiguous per-field copy. Evolution is ADDITIVE-ONLY per the S3 Contract Safety rule (new optional fields may appear at any time; additionalProperties stays open); removing/renaming a required field requires a v2 schema + dual-write window.

schema_version.description: Contract version stamp. Required (unlike signals v1) because this is a new artifact with no legacy unversioned producers to grandfather.

signal_id.description: Stable identifier of the scored signal this outcome resolves (ticker + score_date lineage; producer-defined).

score_date.description: The date the signal was scored (YYYY-MM-DD) — the decision date the horizon is measured forward from.

horizon_days.description: Forward evaluation window in trading days. The canonical primary horizon is 21 (see HorizonPolicy); diagnostic horizons (e.g. 5) are observability only and NOT alpha-equivalent to the primary.

beat_spy.description: Whether the stock's return beat SPY over horizon_days (was beat_spy_{h}d).

stock_return.description: Realized stock return over horizon_days (was return_{h}d).

spy_return.description: Realized SPY return over the same window (was spy_{h}d_return).

log_alpha.description: Log-domain, market-relative, sector-neutral alpha over horizon_days (was log_alpha_{h}d). Populated (non-null) for the canonical primary horizon — see the is_primary conditional below; null-tolerated for diagnostic horizons that carry no canonical alpha.

resolved_at.description: When the outcome was resolved/evaluated (maps from the wide eval_date_{h}d column; a bare date is an acceptable partial timestamp, format is advisory not asserted).

is_primary.description: True iff horizon_days == HorizonPolicy.primary_horizon AND the label matches the canonical label_definition. Lets consumers pick the canonical row without re-deriving the policy.

log_alpha.description: The canonical label MUST be present (non-null) on the primary-horizon record — fail-loud per the no-silent-fails guardrail (config#1483).
